title: cyperus rigens subsp. arechavaletae
intro: Cyperus rigens subsp. arechavaletae, also known as Pajé or Pajé de Céleste, is a subspecies of the Cyperus rigens plant, which is a type of sedge native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cyperus rigens subsp. arechavaletae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
